days.title: Trebuchet?!
days.essence: Extract the first and last digit of each line and sum the two-digit numbers. Part 2 adds spelled-out digit words, and the real subtlety is overlapping words (e.g. 'eightwo') that a naive replace can break.

days.title: Cube Conundrum
days.essence: Parse semicolon/comma-separated cube draws per game, then check feasibility against a bag limit. Part 2 asks for the per-color maxima (minimum viable bag) and the product of those, a light parsing-and-aggregation exercise.

days.title: Gear Ratios
days.essence: On a 2D schematic, find multi-digit numbers adjacent to any symbol; the difficulty is the fiddly bookkeeping of number spans and their 8-neighborhoods. Part 2 reverses the lookup, pairing '*' gears with exactly two adjacent numbers.

days.title: Scratchcards
days.essence: Count matches between winning and held numbers per card. Part 2 turns matches into copies of subsequent cards that cascade, solved cleanly by propagating counts in a single pass rather than literally duplicating cards.

days.title: If You Give A Seed A Fertilizer
days.essence: Chain seeds through layered range-remapping tables to a minimum location. Part 2 reinterprets the seeds as huge ranges, so brute-forcing every seed is infeasible and the intended solution is interval mapping (the user instead brute-forces locations upward to back-solve a valid seed).

days.title: Wait For It
days.essence: Count hold-times that beat each race record, a quadratic distance = hold*(time-hold). Part 2 concatenates the digits into one enormous race, which a closed-form quadratic solves instantly though the values stay small enough to count directly.

days.title: Camel Cards
days.essence: Rank poker-like hands by type then by tie-breaking card order, weighting bids by rank. Part 2 makes J a wildcard, where the optimal play is to greedily add jokers to the largest existing group to maximize hand type.

days.title: Haunted Wasteland
days.essence: Follow L/R instructions through a node network to a target. Part 2 runs many ghosts in parallel and is intractable to simulate jointly; the key insight is each path cycles independently, so the answer is the LCM of the individual cycle lengths.

days.title: Mirage Maintenance
days.essence: Extrapolate sequences via repeated finite differences down to zeros, then build back up. Part 2 just extrapolates backward at the front, a trivial symmetric extension of the same difference recursion.

days.title: Pipe Maze
days.essence: Trace a single pipe loop through the grid; part 1 is just half its length. Part 2 needs the enclosed area (Pick/shoelace or even-odd crossings); the user solves it by upscaling the grid 3x and flood-filling from outside to count interior tiles.

days.title: Cosmic Expansion
days.essence: Sum Manhattan distances between galaxy pairs where empty rows/columns count extra. Part 2 scales empty space by a million, so you cannot expand the grid literally; you count empty-line crossings per pair and multiply, which the user's solution already does.

days.title: Hot Springs
days.essence: Count arrangements of damaged springs matching the run-length groups. Part 1 yields to backtracking (the user's approach), but part 2 unfolds each row five times, making it the canonical memoized-DP day on (string index, group index) state, which is why the user stopped at part 1.

days.title: Point of Incidence
days.essence: Find the row or column of mirror symmetry in each pattern and score it. Part 2 requires the reflection with exactly one differing cell (the smudge), elegantly handled by counting mismatches and accepting the line with a total diff of exactly one.

days.title: Parabolic Reflector Dish
days.essence: Tilt the platform so round rocks roll and slide; part 1 is one northward tilt. Part 2 demands a billion spin cycles, infeasible to simulate, so the answer comes from hashing grid states to detect a repeating period and fast-forwarding.

days.title: Lens Library
days.essence: Apply a simple custom hash ((acc + char) * 17 mod 256) to strings. Part 2 uses that hash to index 256 boxes and faithfully simulates inserting, updating, and removing labeled lenses, then sums focusing power.

days.title: The Floor Will Be Lava
days.essence: Trace a light beam bouncing off mirrors and splitting at splitters, counting energized cells; the key is caching (cell, direction) to terminate the loops that beams create. Part 2 just tries every edge start and takes the max coverage.

days.title: Clumsy Crucible
days.essence: Minimum-heat-loss path on a grid where you cannot go straight more than three cells and cannot reverse, so the Dijkstra state must include direction and run-length. Part 2 (unsolved) changes the crucible to an ultra-crucible with min/max straight-run constraints.

days.title: Lavaduct Lagoon
days.essence: Dig a polygon trench and compute its filled area; the user solves part 1 by rasterizing the grid and flood-filling. Part 2 decodes the hex colors into huge distances, forcing the shoelace formula plus Pick's theorem instead of any grid fill, which is why it is unsolved.

days.title: Aplenty
days.essence: Route parts through a tree of conditional workflow rules; part 1 just runs each part to Accept/Reject. Part 2 asks how many of 4000^4 combos are accepted, requiring you to push x/m/a/s ranges through the rules and split intervals at each comparison.

days.title: Pulse Propagation
days.essence: Simulate flip-flop and conjunction modules passing low/high pulses; part 1 counts pulses over 1000 presses. Part 2 needs the first press that delivers a low pulse to rx, which is astronomically far and only tractable by reverse-engineering the input's independent counter subgraphs and taking their LCM.

days.title: Step Counter
days.essence: BFS the reachable plots after N steps using a parity Set, which the user does for part 1's 64 steps. Part 2 explodes to ~26 million steps on an infinitely tiled garden, solvable only by exploiting the input's open rows/diamond structure and fitting a quadratic to extrapolate the count.

days.title: Sand Slabs
days.essence: Drop 3D brick segments until they settle, then build the supports/supported-by graph. Part 1 counts bricks safe to remove; part 2 sums chain-reaction collapses, a cascading dependency propagation over that support graph.

days.title: A Long Walk
days.essence: Find the longest path through a maze; the crucial move is compressing corridors into a weighted graph of junctions. Part 1's one-way slopes make it a DAG, but part 2 removes them, leaving NP-hard longest-path that requires exhaustive DFS over the contracted graph.

days.title: Never Tell Me The Odds
days.essence: Part 1 counts future XY line intersections of hailstone trajectories inside a box (the user's line-intersection solution). Part 2 finds a single rock position/velocity hitting every hailstone, a nonlinear 3D system of equations / linear-algebra problem, which is why it is unsolved.

days.title: Snowverload
days.essence: Cut the three edges that split the wiring graph into two components and multiply their sizes (a min-cut problem). The user approximates it with Monte-Carlo edge-betweenness, repeatedly sampling shortest paths to find the three most-traversed bridging edges.
